generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot, converting electrical input current (I_M) into mechanical torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω_M) within operational range [-90°, 90°] and max velocity 75°/s."
]

instance GearboxMechanism : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox mechanism integrated with gearwheel, transmitting torque (τ_G) and angular velocity (ω_G) from motor to output shaft while introducing friction effects (static, kinetic, viscous)."
]

instance CNCController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller processing control inputs (position, speed, acceleration) for robot axes and providing reference values (r_M, r_G, r_out) via MQTT streaming."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input motor current (I_M) to enable torque calculation (τ_M) using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on motor shaft measuring angular position (r_M) for backlash and positional uncertainty reduction."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind gearbox measuring angular position (r_G) of gearwheel output for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance MotorPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinition : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing structural composition of first axis components (MotorActuator, GearboxMechanism) and their interconnections (rotational flows)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) illustrating behavioral relationships between motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox including current/torque flows."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance MotorDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data calculation model converting measured current (I_M) into torque (τ_M) using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box analytical friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics for gearbox torque/velocity analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box analytical friction model implementing Stribeck effect for gearbox friction characterization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance BlackBoxFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data-driven black-box friction model trained on operational data for gearbox friction prediction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance IdealGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model simulating gearbox behavior without friction effects (Fig. 4a) for baseline comparison."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance FrictionSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model subtracting friction effects from ideal model to compute deviations (Fig. 4b)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance MotorSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific simulation model (Simulation_M) for motor torque/velocity outputs."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance GearwheelSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific simulation model (Simulation_GW) for gearwheel torque/velocity outputs."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance GearboxSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific simulation model (Simulation_GB) for gearbox torque/velocity outputs."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance MeasuredCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data trace of input motor current (I_M) measured during operation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data trace of motor output angular position (r_M) from motor encoder."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MeasuredGearboxOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data trace of gearbox output angular position (r_G) from secondary encoder."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance SystemOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data trace of system output angular position (r_out) derived from controller."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance SimulatedMotorOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated time-series data trace (δr_M) derived from friction models for motor output."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearboxOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated time-series data trace (δr_G) derived from friction models for gearbox output."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulatedSystemOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated time-series data trace (δr_out) derived from friction models for system output."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance MetadataData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Metadata containing source identification (encoder channels), timestamps, and system properties at data capture time."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted, MotorPositionDataTransmitted, GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Systems Modeling Language) platform enabling structural (BDDs) and behavioral (IBDs) modeling of first axis components and their interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModelingService, BehavioralModelingService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink platform implementing executable friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) and data-driven black-box models for torque/velocity analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService, ComponentSimulationService
]

instance MQTTEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabling real-time streaming of time-series sensor data (I_M, r_M, r_G) from CNC controller to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data processing pipeline aggregating measured and simulated data traces for friction analysis and visualization."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance StructuralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing SysML Block Definition Diagrams for structural composition of first axis components and their interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralInsight
]

instance BehavioralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing SysML Internal Block Diagrams for behavioral relationships between motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously identifying and quantifying joint friction (static, kinetic, viscous) in gearbox by comparing simulated vs. measured outputs."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight, ControlAlgorithmAdjustmentAction
]

instance ComponentSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing component-specific simulations (motor, gearwheel, gearbox) for torque/velocity outputs with friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentBehaviorInsight
]

instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service streaming time-series sensor data (I_M, r_M, r_G) from CNC controller to digital shadow via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service processing and aggregating live data into higher-level abstractions (torque-velocity relationships) for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides AggregatedDataInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service displaying unified dashboard integrating measured vs. simulated data for friction analysis with semantic support and traceability."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance StructuralInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing structural composition of first axis components (motor, gearwheel, gearbox) and their interconnections."
]

instance BehavioralInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight illustrating behavioral relationships between motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox including current/torque flows."
]

instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight quantifying static, kinetic, and viscous friction in gearbox by comparing simulated (friction-free) vs. measured outputs."
]

instance ComponentBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing torque/velocity outputs for individual components (motor, gearwheel, gearbox) with friction effects."
]

instance DataTraceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing time-series data traces (I_M, r_M, r_G) with source identification and timestamps for analysis."
]

instance AggregatedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing higher-level abstractions of torque-velocity relationships derived from processed data."
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing unified dashboard visualization of measured vs. simulated data with friction deviations highlighted."
]

instance ControlAlgorithmAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action enabling adjustment of control algorithms to compensate for identified friction effects and improve motion accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance MaintenanceSchedulingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action informing maintenance scheduling based on friction trends and wear analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ProcessOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action providing recommendations for process optimization by adjusting machining parameters to compensate for friction losses."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C18: Environment
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxMechanism, CNCController,
                 MotorCurrentSensor, MotorPositionEncoder, GearboxPositionEncoder,
                 SysMLBlockDefinition, SysMLInternalBlockDiagram,
                 MotorDynamicsModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel,
                 StribeckFrictionModel, BlackBoxFrictionModel,
                 IdealGearboxModel, FrictionSimulationModel,
                 MotorSimulation, GearwheelSimulation, GearboxSimulation
]

// C19: Components
instance FirstAxisAsset : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "First vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot comprising permanent magnet synchronous motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox operating within [-90°, 90°] range."
]

instance FrictionShadow : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital shadow component implementing friction analysis models (white-box and black-box) for gearbox friction characterization."
]

instance DataTraceComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component managing data traces (measured and simulated) for friction analysis with source metadata and timestamps."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital shadow operates on a **real-time, continuous time scale**, synchronized with the operational cycle of the industrial robot. Key time-related aspects include:
   - **Data acquisition frequency**: Sensor data (e.g., encoders, current) is streamed at a frequency sufficient to capture dynamic behavior of the first axis, though exact sampling rates are not specified in the document.
   - **Model computation latency**: Simulation models (e.g., Simulink-based) process data in near-real-time to enable **dynamic exchange of friction models** and immediate visualization of results.
   - **Dashboard updates**: The unified dashboard provides **up-to-date visualizations** of friction-related deviations, enabling operators to monitor and analyze performance continuously during robot operation."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is organized as follows:
   - **Centralized DS Architecture**: A single, **executable digital shadow** focuses on the **first axis** of the MABI MAX 100 robot, integrating:
     - **Physical Components**: Motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox, encoders, and Siemens controller.
     - **Virtual Components**:
       - **Engineering Models** (SysML): Describe asset structure and behavior.
       - **Simulation Models** (Simulink): Compute friction deviations.
       - **Processing Components**: Execute data calculations (e.g., torque/velocity derivation) and simulations (e.g., friction modeling).
     - **Data Flow**: One-way from PT (via MQTT) to DS, with **no autonomous feedback** to the physical system.
   - **Orchestration**:
     - The DS is **purpose-specific** (friction analysis) and **scope-limited** to the first axis, though the methodology supports extension to other axes or robots.
     - **Traceability**: Data points are linked to their **sources** (e.g., encoders), **models** (e.g., friction simulations), and **processing steps** (e.g., torque calculation) via metadata and UML associations."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital shadow operates primarily during the **operational phase** of the robot’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** representations of the physical twin (PT). It does not explicitly cover:
   - **As-designed** (idealized) or **as-manufactured** phases, though engineering models (e.g., SysML Block Definition Diagrams) describe the **structural and behavioral design** of the first axis.
   The DS is **purpose-built** for **real-time friction analysis** during active use, enabling **continuous model refinement** based on operational data."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity of the digital shadow are ensured through:
   - **Model Accuracy**:
     - **White-box models** (analytical friction models like Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) incorporate **load, temperature, and time-dependent effects** but require parameter tuning.
     - **Black-box models** (data-driven) avoid parameter identification but may lack interpretability.
     - **Simulation models** (Simulink) are validated against **measured data traces** to quantify deviations caused by friction.
   - **Verification and Validation**:
     - **Data traceability**: Each data point is linked to its **source** (e.g., encoder channel) and **processing steps** (e.g., torque calculation), ensuring reproducibility.
     - **Uncertainty quantification**: Deviations between **simulated (ˆ*r_M*, ˆ*r_G*)** and **measured (*r_M*, *r_G*)** outputs highlight friction losses and model inaccuracies.
     - **Error sources**: Backlash, encoder noise, and **gearbox elasticity** introduce uncertainties in torque/velocity measurements.
   - **Uncertainty Handling**: The DS does not explicitly address **uncertainty propagation** or **error correction**, but the **comparison of simulated vs. measured data** provides a basis for identifying and mitigating inaccuracies."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital shadow (DS) involves:
   - **Data Transmission Protocol**: **MQTT** is used to stream **time-series sensor data** (e.g., encoder readings, current) from the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller** to the DS.
   - **Network Architecture**: The DS operates as a **passive receiver** of data, with no explicit mention of **real-time communication protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, EtherCAT) for bidirectional control. The connection is **one-way**, aligned with the **digital shadow definition** (Kritzinger et al.).
   - **Data Format**: Sensor data is structured into **DataTraces** (e.g., *I_M*, *r_M*), each containing arrays of **torque (*τ*) and angular velocity (*ω*)** as floating-point values. Metadata includes **source identification** (e.g., encoder channel) and **time stamps**."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital shadow is **hosted and deployed** in a **software environment** that integrates:
   - **Simulation Platform**: **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** runs the **executable friction and dynamics models**.
   - **Data Processing**: The DS processes data traces using **custom processing components** (e.g., *Simulation_M*, *Simulation_GW*) implemented in Simulink.
   - **Visualization**: A **unified dashboard** (likely web-based or desktop application) displays integrated data for friction analysis.
   - **Deployment Context**: The DS runs on a **local or cloud-based server** connected to the Siemens controller via MQTT. The document does not specify **scalability** (e.g., multi-robot deployment) or **hardware requirements** (e.g., CPU, memory)."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The digital shadow does **not explicitly describe horizontal integration** with external information systems or other digital twins. However, the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework (implied in the methodology) could facilitate integration with:
   - **Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES)**: For broader process optimization.
   - **Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)**: To align robot performance with production planning.
   - **Other Digital Twins**: If extended to multiple robots or axes, the DS could share friction models or data via standardized interfaces (e.g., OPC UA)."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "The document does **not explicitly address data ownership or privacy**. However, based on the context:
   - **Data Ownership**: Likely resides with the **robot operator or manufacturer** (MABI Robotic AG), as the DS aggregates data from the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller** and **sensor systems**.
   - **Privacy Considerations**: No mention of **data anonymization**, **access controls**, or **compliance with regulations** (e.g., GDPR). The DS focuses on **internal use** for friction analysis, with no indication of sharing sensitive operational data externally."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin system adheres to the following standards and frameworks:
   - **Modeling Standards**:
     - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: Used for **structural and behavioral modeling** of the first axis (BDDs, IBDs).
     - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Implied as a conceptual framework for **modeling and linking data**, though not explicitly implemented in this use case.
   - **Simulation Standards**:
     - **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink**: Industry-standard platform for **executable simulation models** (friction, dynamics).
   - **Data Exchange**:
     - **MQTT**: Lightweight messaging protocol for **time-series data streaming**.
   - **Industry-Specific Standards**: The methodology aligns with **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** practices (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) for complex system modeling."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The document does **not explicitly address security or safety considerations**, but the following can be inferred:
   - **Security**:
     - **No remote control**: The digital shadow is **passive** and does not execute operations remotely on the PT, reducing risks of unauthorized commands.
     - **Data Transmission**: MQTT is used for data streaming, which may lack **end-to-end encryption** or **authentication** by default. Vulnerabilities could arise if the network is compromised.
     - **Access Control**: No mention of **role-based access** or **data encryption** for the dashboard or processing components.
   - **Safety**:
     - **No autonomous feedback**: The DS does not send commands back to the PT, mitigating risks of **accidents or human injury** from automated corrections.
     - **Operational Monitoring**: The dashboard provides **real-time insights** into friction, which could aid in **predictive maintenance** and **safety-critical decision-making** (e.g., stopping the robot if friction exceeds thresholds).
   - **Ethical Considerations**: The DS is designed for **internal use** (friction analysis), with no indication of **human-in-the-loop safety protocols** or **emergency shutdown mechanisms**."
]
